title: genista versicolor subsp. pumila
intro: Genista versicolor subsp. pumila, also known as dwarf broom or dwarf purple broom, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the pea family and native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health, reducing stress, and aiding in digestion, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Genista versicolor subsp. pumila is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
